evals.prompt: The ticket plugin at packages/plugins/ticket/ doesn't have a CHANGELOG yet. Can you create one? It's had several versions — look at the git history and PRs to piece together the full release history.
evals.expected_output: A CHANGELOG.md file at packages/plugins/ticket/CHANGELOG.md following Keep a Changelog format, with version sections derived from git history and PRs (approximately v1.0.0 through v1.3.x), each with categorized entries (Added/Changed/Fixed) that reference PR numbers where applicable.

assertions.text: CHANGELOG.md file exists in outputs directory
Uses Keep a Changelog format: has '# Changelog' header and version sections with '## [X.Y.Z]' pattern
Contains at least 3 distinct version sections (ticket plugin has had ~4+ versions)
At least 2 entries reference PR numbers using (#N) format
Uses standard categories (Added, Fixed, Changed) — at least 2 different categories present
Covers key ticket plugin features: guard hook, /ticket skill or triage, and engine hardening
No entries that describe features or changes that don't exist in the actual codebase

evals.prompt: Audit the handoff plugin CHANGELOG at packages/plugins/handoff/CHANGELOG.md — check if anything is missing or inaccurate by comparing it against the git history and merged PRs.
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report listing: (1) entries verified as accurate, (2) any missing changes not captured in the CHANGELOG, (3) any inaccurate or miscategorized entries, (4) formatting issues. Should cross-reference against git log and gh pr list for the handoff plugin.

assertions.text: Audit report file exists in outputs directory
Report has distinct sections for verified entries, missing entries, and inaccurate entries
Report mentions specific commits, PRs, or git history as evidence
Identifies at least one concrete finding (missing entry, inaccuracy, or formatting issue)
Each finding includes enough detail to act on (what's wrong and what it should be)

evals.prompt: The cross-model plugin changelog needs updating — there have been changes since the 2.0.0 release on March 1st. Can you add entries for everything that's happened since then under the [Unreleased] section?
evals.expected_output: Updated CHANGELOG.md with new entries under [Unreleased] covering all changes since 2026-03-01, gathered from git log, PRs, and handoff files. Entries should be categorized and reference PR numbers.

assertions.text: Updated CHANGELOG.md file exists in outputs directory
Existing 2.0.0 and 1.0.0 sections are preserved intact
New entries are under an [Unreleased] section at the top
New entries only cover changes after 2026-03-01 (no duplicates of 2.0.0 entries)
New entries reference PR numbers where applicable
New entries match the writing style and granularity of existing entries
